prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene was performed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1.

The calculation predicts the most electrophilic and nucleophilic sites in the molecule, which are indicators of reactivity.
The site most susceptible to a nucleophilic attack is atom C4, with a Fukui(+) index of 0.49642.
The site most susceptible to an electrophilic attack is atom C5, with a Fukui(-) index of 0.10729.

The calculation was completed successfully and passed all integrity checks.
